channel.title: Benchmark PS Blog
channel.description: Practical insights on physiotherapy outcomes, MSK measurement, and evidence-based practice.

item.title: The Business Case for Outcome Measurement in Physiotherapy Clinics
item.description: Outcome measurement is not an administrative burden — it is a direct lever on clinic profitability, patient retention, and staff performance. Here is the evidence.

item.title: How to Implement Objective Physiotherapy Assessment Without Expensive Equipment
item.description: Over 90% of physiotherapists rely on subjective strength testing. Here is how to build a consistent, evidence-based objective assessment protocol using low-cost tools.

item.title: MSK Outcome Benchmarks: What Good Conservative Care Actually Looks Like
item.description: What does a good physiotherapy outcome actually look like in the evidence? This article maps published benchmarks across the most common MSK conditions.

item.title: Evidence-Based Progression in Physiotherapy: A Framework for Time-Pressured Clinicians
item.description: Progression decisions in physiotherapy are often made on feel. Here is a structured, evidence-based framework for deciding when and how to progress patients.

item.title: Why 43% of Physiotherapy Care Is Non-Recommended — and What Clinics Can Do About It
item.description: Systematic reviews show that nearly half of physiotherapy care delivered does not follow evidence-based guidelines. This article explains why and what practices can do about it.

item.title: Staffing Mix and Physiotherapy Margins: How Standardised Care Reduces Senior Clinician Dependency
item.description: Labour accounts for 60–70% of outpatient physiotherapy costs. Here is how standardising clinical assessment and progression decisions changes the economics of staffing.

item.title: ACL Return-to-Sport Testing: Which Criteria Actually Predict Re-Injury Risk
item.description: Time-based ACL return-to-sport protocols produce re-injury rates of 15–25%. Here is what the evidence says about criteria-based testing and how to implement it.

item.title: The State of Technology in Physiotherapy: What Works, What Doesn't, and What's Coming
item.description: Force plates, isokinetic dynamometers, and wearables have all promised to transform physiotherapy. Here is an honest assessment of what the evidence supports.

item.title: The Economic Case for Better MSK Conservative Care
item.description: MSK conditions cost the UK economy over £5bn annually. Improving physiotherapy effectiveness is one of the most cost-effective interventions available. Here is the evidence.

item.title: Knee Osteoarthritis and Conservative Care: Setting Realistic Expectations With Objective Data
item.description: Knee OA is the most common musculoskeletal condition in adults over 50. Here is what the evidence says about conservative care outcomes and how to communicate them accurately.
